title: Talk With Prudence To A Beggar
content:
Talk with prudence to a beggar
    Of 'Potosi' and the mines!
    Reverently to the hungry
    Of your viands and your wines!

    Cautious, hint to any captive
    You have passed enfranchised feet!
    Anecdotes of air in dungeons
    Have sometimes proved deadly sweet!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Talk With Prudence To A Beggar"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
